meta.title: How to Allocate to Digital Assets: The Menu of Options Available to Institutional LPs

transcript:
Good morning, everybody. I hope you're doing well. So, yeah, Raoul just set sort of the stage for why invest in digital assets at all. Why does it deserve an allocation? This is really for institutions to understand the options out there once you understand why it is worth an allocation. So I'm Pierre Henry. I work with Raoul every day at EXPAAM as a partner there, and we help institutional allocators understand where digital assets sit in a multi-asset portfolio. So we have about 30 minutes today, and my goal is to really help you understand what's out there, create a taxonomy for you. And so we'll run through the state of crypto today. I'm going to reinforce some of the messages Raoul brought up. We're going to run through portfolio composition and then the menu of options out there. And hopefully, you'll be left realizing that this place, that crypto deserves a spot there.

Um, so we love to think about, as Raoul said, where we are. We're about 3% of the way through this journey. And actually, there will be, over the next 10 years, more wealth than the internet, cloud, and mobile combined. So it is absolutely one of the key exponential technologies to invest in today. And what we've done at EXPAAM is we have normalized the growth in crypto users with the internet from a 5 million user base. And what you see from the chart is that from 2016 to 2021, crypto was growing at about 137% every year. And it's about double the rate of the internet. The reason for that is it is building on top of the internet. So it has things the internet never had in its early days, right? Everything from social media to digital virality to larger scale networks, digital broadband, mobile, global mobile usage around the world. And even during the multi-year bear market, it was growing at 43%, which is the same as the internet in its main adoption phase.

And basically, you know, where we are today is we're at about a billion users. And from the trend rate of growth, we expect to be at 3 to 4 billion by 2030. So this is an unstoppable train. You saw this chart before, but I do want to re-highlight this, which is that crypto dominates the return stacks. And this chart looks at really the start of crypto in 2011 through October 31st of this year. And as you can see, it's not even close. Bitcoin is 36 and a half million percent growth. Ethereum over 400,000. And Solana, although it's younger, is well over 10,000 percent. So the key question is, do these things create diversification in a portfolio, efficiency in a portfolio, or is this just sort of a random power law example? And this is something we're going to talk about.

And it's very interesting because institutions today regularly express very strong interest in investing into the space, but the allocation doesn't reflect that interest. And if you are a student of financial history and you've seen things like private credit or hedge funds in their early days, emerging markets, it's always the same thing. Allocation lags interest. And basically, nobody does anything until the operational risks and the regulation are taken care of. And what we've seen is crypto is in exactly that spot today. So this is a good forward-looking indicator. It's kind of a really good moment to invest. And so I thought I would go over the typical concerns I hear from institutions about why this space is not worth an investment and how they have been addressed.

First is the regulatory uncertainty. And really, as we will go over on the next slide, that has completely changed. There really is comprehensive frameworks around the world now and cooperative governments, policy. Safety of platforms. 2022, of course, there was tons of issues with custody, counterparty exchange risk. And that's all been resolved now. Now we have everything from SOC audited accounts to segregated accounts to institutional-grade custody and insurance. And so there is real safety in the platforms. Obviously, the famous volatility of the space. You couldn't go to a risk committee in past years because everyone would say the volatility is too high. But actually, what we've seen over the data over the past few years is that on a risk-adjusted basis, you are paid for your volatility. And in fact, by sizing appropriately, you can deal with the volatility very easily.

The last big comment was always, you know, is there value beyond speculation? And we do know today that, and you'll hear about this actually throughout Breakpoint from other practitioners and entrepreneurs, that there are clear fundamental and economic drivers in this space that make a lot of sense. Everything from stablecoins to rollups to corporate adoption. So I thought I can't go through everything with the 30 minutes, but I wanted to highlight a few of the key things over the last few years. When Ethereum completed in real time the Merge, it showed that a major protocol could upgrade without breaking. And basically, it shifted to proof of stake, which created institutional-grade yield for the first time. And to this day, it's reduced 99% of the energy usage, and it sort of set the precedent for others to follow.

MiCA came out and created a full framework in Europe to understand stablecoins, custodians, exchanges, disclosures. So that was a very big deal. The Ripple case was deemed, you know, it was the first time we had judicial guardrails to say when is something a security or not. Obviously, it was deemed to not be a security, secondary sales were not securities. And this really started to push back on the "everything is a security" narrative of the SEC. Solana, of course, has been recognized for its unbelievable throughput by leading payment providers as a chosen partner for payments infrastructure. And it's only getting faster and faster every single year.

The Trump administration has created a bipartisan innovation agenda, and it's allowed policy and regulation in the US to come forward. And the biggest things lately have been the Genius Act, which provide a clear rules of the road style framework for how stablecoins work, how safe harbor works, and basically when you file for any type of product, what the rules should be in the first place. Same thing with Project Crypto. SEC has moved from an adversarial to a collaborative stance. And long-yearned-for transparent rules of the road around classification of ETFs and filings have finally been approved. So it's really helped the industry at large.

And so where we are today is well beyond speculation. We've sort of achieved an industry defined by scale. So we have $46 trillion annual stablecoin transaction volume, 70 million monthly active users. So these are real people. These are unique individuals who are borrowing, lending, swapping, trading on exchanges and creating a real economy in real time. There's now $175 billion in ETPs and institutional wrappers around the world. And according to the surveys that have been conducted here, 70% of the respondents recognize that this is an opportunity with huge convexity, right? There's much more upside than downside. And finally, most have recognized that it does provide a very clear role in portfolio efficiency and diversification.

So as Raoul had referenced, this thing is so small. We're only three and a half trillion dollars, three trillion depending on the day, versus the other major asset classes out there. You can see that the white space is truly enormous in front of us. So why do crypto strategies exist? And what's structural about them that create these persistent opportunities? So, you know, crypto is payments, money, and settlement in one programmable layer. And that creates alpha that doesn't exist anywhere else. You won't find in traditional markets. Everything from blockspace to staking to MEV, which is our term for extracting profit from the ordering of transactions on a chain.

Okay, there is extreme volatility, but it's structured. Because markets are 24/7 and you see these big moves in the sigma, you see these standard deviation changes that Raoul referenced, in reality, this is the base material for market-neutral basis trades, options premia, and strategies associated with it. The market structure is developing in real time. And what you'll find is that liquidity is fragmented across venues. And because of that, arbitrage and high-frequency trading can exist in a way that doesn't in mature equity or foreign exchange markets anymore. So we've seen strategies grow around that.

Finally, because everything is on-chain and transparent and audited, you can actually do unbelievable quant-style strategies and models that didn't exist in the past. Everything from factor models to wallet cluster analysis to various on-chain metrics. And because with the liquid token market, the majority of returns are being powered by just a handful of projects, you do get this power law dynamic. So it makes a lot of sense for venture models to come into the space, especially on the liquid side. And then finally, as referenced, we have now real institutional-grade yield that has come into the space. And everything from neutral income strategies to digital asset treasuries to ETFs with staking built into them have entered the scene.

So here's the stuff that I get the most interested in, which is that this is a five-year lookback so that we could include Solana from its inception. And basically, there is a persistent low correlation between crypto and most of the asset classes that institutions care about. And so some of these things like bonds and commodities, the correlation is almost zero. And it's interesting because actually those are the two categories that institutions tend to lack diversification in the most when they do a 60/40 portfolio. And so when you see this type of data on the screen, it makes you really wonder, okay, then the math of a 60/40 portfolio should actually improve dramatically if you add a little bit of crypto.

And so we did the math in-house at EXPAAM. And on this chart, what you're seeing is Bitcoin, Ethereum, and Solana. And what happens to a 60/40 portfolio when you add the various cryptos to it. So the X on the bottom left is the normal 60/40 portfolio. The return on the Y-axis, the volatility on the X-axis. And what we see in the dashed lines are the Sharpe ratios associated with each of these. And so the math is very straightforward. If you add 5%, up to 5% of Bitcoin, you are improving the returns without an offset increase in volatility. It's about 1 to 2% in Ethereum and it's about 3 to 4% in Solana. And so the volatility you think would go up by several percentage points, it tends to be tens of basis points. And this is well within institutional tolerances for volatility. So even adding 1% makes a huge difference. And I think that if institutions do the math, they'll start to realize that advising clients to do this makes a lot of sense. And so we are starting to see Bank of America and other private banks, Morgan Stanley, offer crypto to clients and advise these sorts of percentage ranges, which is really good.

But what I also want to focus on is the downside ratios. You know, everybody wants to use a Sharpe ratio, but the problem with the Sharpe ratio is that you get penalized for all volatility, including upside volatility, which crypto has plenty of. So what I care about is sort of understanding how much return do you get per unit of pain, per downside volatility. And so when you look at a Sortino ratio, which is measuring the downside, or a Calmar, which looks at the max drawdown, what you find is that you're compensated. You get much better returns in crypto than even in equities. And so, bonds, for instance, which tend to make up most of the portfolios of institutions, you get the worst score of all. So despite these big moves up and down, on a risk-adjusted basis, you're well compensated. And so although volatility is important to consider, it's really the nature of the volatility that institutions should wonder about. And these charts help set the record straight.

So a quick taxonomy of how to think about the strategies. At a high level, we've created six. There's your sort of directional exposure by owning spot directly or something like an ETF. There's the active component of liquid investing, so kind of active management, working with a hedge fund, because they have all the time professionally to do the research and they have access to projects earlier than most people. You have the staking, the digital asset treasuries that provide an alternative to spot for mandates for institutions. You have, of course, structured products, things that usually have a defined downside or a certain amount of yield that's consistent that appeals to risk-sensitive capital. Private markets, so the venture, the growth, both on the equity and the token side. And then market neutral. So these are the uncorrelated returns that extract efficiencies out of the market regardless of market direction. And this is the kind of taxonomy institutions should operate with.

And you can actually use the same instruments in TradFi in crypto. So if you look at liquidity tradeoffs across these strategies, we can layer that in there now. So the highest liquid stuff would be the tokenized RWAs or things like T-bills, the perpetuals, the wrappers. And then you get your staking and your separately managed accounts, which are more on a weekly basis. The market neutral guys, the macro hedge funds, these tend to be monthly in nature. Your credit and directional hedge fund strategies tend to be quarterly. And then, of course, you have venture and growth on the illiquid side. And so what we find is that if we use this framework, we can help allocators figure out what their mandate is and then where to express it. And if you are willing to balance liquidity with returns, with a tradeoff in liquidity, you can actually create a really interesting view of the world and a curve. And you can express what kind of return you want using this methodology.

And so to explain in a little bit more detail, basically on the left side, you have spot liquid tokens, which are... this is an index from the S&P top 10 equally weighted over the last five years. And you can see it's coming in at about 18% per year. But as you move along, you start to get much better returns. So you have the market neutral guys kind of hovering above 20%. So these guys will very consistently produce these returns, and that tends to be a defensive position that institutions like. And then the fundamental hedge funds, which are the groups we work with very often, they produce by far the highest returns, but they suffer from the full downside volatility of the cycle. So what we do at EXPAAM is we manage cycle risk. We use the macro framework that Raoul walked you through, and we will invest in the directional guys in high liquidity environments. And then as we get into environments where the liquidity contracts, we'll go down to the market neutral side. And what ends up happening is over the course of the full cycle, you capture the full exponential technology trend, but you reduce as much of the downside as possible. And that's how we help our clients get the best returns possible. So this framework is perfect for institutions to map out where they want to be and why.

So what we do say in the end for our clients is that the best strategy is actually to take a barbell approach. And so on the left, you have your sort of blue-chip capital preservation. So this is like Bitcoin or Ethereum. And the idea here is you're highly liquid, you can control the risk yourself, you don't have to pay a manager a fee. And in the process, you know, you have lower volatility, you have a higher market cap project, usually something that makes an investment committee or a family office feel more comfortable. But if you don't have convexity in the portfolio, you're missing out on the secular growth of the space. And so on the right side, you have your smaller cap projects. And this is very much like investing in smaller market cap equities. It's really hard. Token selection is just like that. It's very difficult. And so you do need specialists who spend all of their time doing this. So if you only are owning Bitcoin and Ethereum, you're not getting the full picture. And we find by recommending a 50/50 or a 60/40 or a 70/30 split, you're actually going to have the best efficiency in your portfolio with your crypto allocation.

So this is the language of most of the multi-asset managers. So we tried to translate this onto what they would be used to seeing. So we're going to run through now the menu of options and fit them into one of these buckets: growth, return-seeking, illiquid alternatives, defensive, or income and liquidity. So, of course, not everyone can hold spot, but when you do, it's by far the purest expression of your belief in this secular technology trend. Obviously, it is clean, it tends to be easily custodied, segregated. You have NAV reporting which comes very easily. And at the end of the day, you're getting exposed to the full upside and downside of the cycle. So for something like Solana, if you can pick something like that, you'll get an unbelievable CAGR, but you'll get high volatility in the process, but be paid for the volatility. So this fits into the growth bucket.

The problem is that most institutions can't hold spot. So they go for the ETFs because they are familiar, they can be traded in a standard brokerage account, and effectively, you know, still offer the same benefits of liquidity. So that would be the second option. And these days, the staking is being built directly into the ETF. So this is again a growth option. Staking, which for those who don't know, is when you essentially delegate your voting power of the units that you hold to a validator who then helps with consensus on a proof-of-stake network. And so this is the third option for institutions out there. And what we see across all the networks is you get anywhere from 2 to 12% in the staking. In an environment with falling rates, this is particularly exciting and interesting. So it would be an income and growth mandate.

And finally, you get your more capital-efficient expression of beta, which will come from perpetuals, which are a type of futures contract which roll over, there's no end date, and basis trades. And so it's a way of essentially hedging without changing the underlying portfolio, but you are subject to high volatility. So we put it into the return-seeking bucket. Then you get into hedge funds, right? So this is where we live most of the time. These are the guys who do the research and are able to combine narrative with product roadmaps and investment and, you know, effectively looking at teams and looking at where distribution works and all the things that might go into understanding an equity but applying it to crypto. You do trade that for a higher lockup. And throughout the manager database that we have, you have huge dispersion in performance. So in bear markets, we've seen some of the directional guys fall 80%, but then in the bull markets, they'll be up over 1,000% in a year. So this is more of a return-seeking option.

The market neutral guys, as I mentioned, are making money regardless of which way the market moves. They use everything from statistical arbitrage to liquidity provisioning to DeFi activity to market making, and they're able to create a very consistent type of return. So this would be your defensive complement to some of the more liquid directional options. And on credit and lending, this is effectively, because there isn't a mature prime brokerage ecosystem in crypto, you're able to get very high yield premia. And so you're seeing versus TradFi very attractive numbers that are persistent. And it's characterized mostly by stablecoin lending and overcollateralized positions. So this is in your income bucket as well.

And multi-strat is one of the last hedge fund options. This is, of course, probably very familiar for institutions. Very flexible. These are guys who will change their approach depending on the macro regime. This is similar to what we do, where they will combine relative value, options, rates, macro overlays, and effectively dynamically move along the risk curve. So you will see that they generally have lower volatility, but in exchange, they give up some of the returns you'll see from the directional guys.

Okay, and then I can't be at Breakpoint and not mention the Solana-specific stuff. So this is actually, doesn't exist in TradFi. These are funds that take advantage of the high throughput, low latency of Solana. And they effectively are capturing alpha with things that are unique to blockchains like this. So something like atomic settlement where you have an interdependent exchange of assets and you remove settlement risk. You use on-chain order books, strategies related to that. And so this is sort of growth and return together in one place and exhibits the same volatility as a directional strategy.

On the private market side, you have venture, which everyone will be I think much more familiar with, both on the equity and the liquid token side. On the equity side, you're obviously participating in power laws. So these are, you know, portfolios where a fraction of the investments actually generate the majority of returns. And it is absolutely about access. Managers who get allocations first generally are going to outperform retail and certainly even professional investors. And as the space evolves, this whole illiquid alternatives universe, we're seeing more and more on the growth side. So these are your application-style, whether it's an analytics company, a two-sided marketplace, a SaaS company. These are the companies that have clearly defined unit economics, they have product-market fit, they're starting to scale, they're entering the first challenges that happen with corporate finance, with M&A, with entering new markets, with CapEx. And there's a whole ecosystem growing around this. These guys have faster exits, right? But they tend to have also lower returns. Their loss ratios are lower as well. And so for institutions that want to balance the volatility, they don't want volatility in part of the portfolio, this is where they should be looking.

Ecosystem funds are really just specialized, right? So these are things like Multicoin focuses on Solana, they're very famous for that. I'm going to be talking to Tushar later today. And so if you want access to a specific theme that you're trying to monetize or you want a specific protocol, this is the way to go. And happy to chat with anyone about that afterwards. Finally, we've got the wrappers. I'm trying to move along here with the time we have. Of course, digital asset treasuries are famous. You know, if you can find a management team with high brand equity and high velocity, high liquidity, then they can issue shares accretively and you get amplified exposure to the underlying, both on the upside and the downside. And actually, for a lot of institutions that can't own spot, this has been the way to go to express a view.

Tokenized securities are what they sound like. It's when you offer a fund structure as a token on-chain. Basically, these are for accredited investors. And you're going to have all the benefits of blockchain with your normal subscription process. So if anybody's invested in a fund admin before, that whole process goes away. You have instant settlement, you have global distribution, you don't have the same paperwork. And all the compliance is programmable. So this would be again in the return-seeking liquidity bucket. Structured yield is probably the most familiar actually of all of these for institutions. This is when you have basically a defined downside and a defined upside product. For risk-sensitive capital, this becomes extremely interesting and attractive. Especially in sideways markets because there's a lot of volatility so you can monetize the options premia very, very well. So that's an income-related strategy.

And then to wrap up on the menu of options, you have tokenized RWAs, which are more like money market funds, tokenized treasuries. Focus more on yield streams than on absolute returns, I would say. Obviously low volatility, low returns, but a very nice component to amplify and stabilize the rest of the portfolio. So last couple of slides here. I just thought it would be helpful to run through what's different when you do due diligence in crypto versus TradFi, since that is a question we get all of the time. Custody is the obvious first option. Unlike TradFi where it's commoditized, here you really have to worry about private keys, the signing process, and the operational risk around that.

Counterparty risk. So in TradFi, of course, custodians, exchanges, and fund admins are all separate. Here they tend to blend together in crypto. So you need to worry much more, of course, about rehypothecation and the venue you're in and exactly where you're storing your money. Tokenomics is completely replacing sort of capital structure analysis, leverage ratios, cash flow analysis. This is much more about the quality of the tokenomics of the project, the distribution, the team, the amount of money being invested. So it's a totally different framework that is unique to crypto. As I mentioned, liquidity is fragmented across all of these venues. And so, you know, where you're investing and the timing of the investment matter very, very much.

Technology risk is as important as financial due diligence in our world. You have to absolutely have a sense of basically the quality of the protocol. And we see exchange hacks and corrupted tokens have created problems in the past. So this is a huge area of due diligence. Regulatory risk, just understanding tax implications, what the rules of the roads are in various jurisdictions. That's important. Operational controls is pretty much the same as TradFi for us. It's focusing on the quality of the operating team. And actually, most of the hedge funds we analyze don't make the cut because they fail in this category. Risk sizing is super important because the volatility is so high. You know, the mistakes can get amplified very quickly. So arguably this matters as much as protocol selection. And finally, the team matters in a young industry where you don't have long track records you can audit. So if a team understands distributed systems, cryptography, and has a clear investment process, that becomes a huge focus for us.

Okay, so in summary, the main takeaways are that, you know, we are on a one-way train to billions of users. And the internet and the growth of the internet is a really useful early model for that. There is a huge gap today between interest and allocation, but this has always been historically across asset classes where the biggest returns have been made. There is a full spectrum of institutional strategies that are out there for institutions to express their view along. And even a small, even 1% allocation in any of the blue-chip cryptocurrencies can push out the efficient frontier of a portfolio. All of this is underwritable now across the frameworks we went through. And basically, there are compliance structures today that didn't exist in the past for institutions to make investments in. So kind of a whirlwind, but that's the summary. It's early on, 3% of the way through the journey. We love questions at EXPAAM. We love to be helpful. So if anyone would like to reach out, my email is pierre@expaam.com. And would be happy to, whether you're a professional or an institution or even retail, direct you to the right place. And thank you so much for your time. Next we have Paolo from our team who's going to be interviewing some of our managers.

The provided transcript snippet does not mention any specific product being launched by EXPAAM at Solana Breakpoint 2025 for which an Official Announcement Blog or Technical Docs would exist. Therefore, a specific product name cannot be extracted from this text.

However, to provide a query string as requested, I will use the company name and event, searching for general announcements or documentation related to their presence, while acknowledging the absence of a specific product name from the given text.

sources.title: Plume Brings Institutional Real-World Yield to Solana With Launch of RWA Nest Vaults
sources.snippet:
Plume Brings Institutional Real-World Yield to Solana With Launch of RWA Nest Vaults[Accessibility Statement](https://www.cision.com/about/accessibility/)[Skip Navigation](#main)
# Plume Brings Institutional Real-World Yield to Solana With Launch of RWA Nest Vaults
[![Plume Network Logo (PRNewsfoto/Plume Network)]( "Plume Network Logo (PRNewsfoto/Plume Network)")](#)
## News provided by
[**Plume Network**](https://www.prnewswire.com/news/plume-network/)
Dec 05, 2025, 09:15 ET

*Nest vaults with assets from WisdomTree, Hamilton Lane, BlackOpal and issuers including Securitize and SuperState mark the beginning of a new yield economy forSolana.*
NEW YORK,Dec. 5, 2025/PRNewswire/ --[Plume](https://edge.prnewswire.com/c/link/?t=0&l=en&o=4573545-1&h=1349188964&u=https://plume.org/&a=Plume), the leading RWA blockchain powering real-world yield, today announced the launch of five RWA Nest vaults onSolana, setting real-world yield as a new base layer for the ecosystem and marking an important first step in Plume's multichain expansion for Nest.
Launching the vaults natively onSolanagives users direct investment opportunities to institutional-grade assets from WisdomTree, Hamilton Lane, BlackOpal, and issuers including Securitize and SuperState.Solanausers can natively access onchain credit, treasuries, and short-term receivables and the ability to earn institutional-grade real-world yield.
With the launch of nBASIS, nOPAL, nWISDOM, nALPHA and nTBILL Vaults,Solana's 20+ million users can soon deposit stablecoins natively into Nest to unlock reliable, stable, real-world APY. Each deposit returns a Nest vaulttoken, a liquid, yield-bearing asset that accrues value over time and can move seamlessly acrossSolanaDeFi, from DEX liquidity pools to lending and structured products, with the ability to redeem at any point.
As part of this integration, Plume is also launching a deep partnership with[Squads Lab](https://edge.prnewswire.com/c/link/?t=0&l=en&o=4573545-1&h=2271106279&u=https://squads.xyz/&a=Squads+Lab),Solana's leading multi-sig and treasury management infrastructure provider. Plume will introduce its RWA yield pipelines across the Squads ecosystem, including a bespoke vault integrated for Altitude users and seamless RWA yield access for developers building with Grid.
*"Cryptois moving past the era of synthetic yield and into a market grounded in real economics. Stablecoins brought millions intocrypto, but yieldcoins will keep them here. This integration withSolanais the next step in building a transparent, programmable financial system at scale."*said Teddy Pornprinya, Co-Founder and CBO at Plume.
*"Plume already anchors over half of the industry's RWA activity, andSolanahas become one of the most efficient, high-throughput environments incrypto, making it a natural home for onchain credit, treasuries, and short-term receivables. Bringing our strengths together is how we unlock the next phase of onchain finan*ce".
By introducing institutional RWAs at scale, Plume is expanding theSolanaDeFiecosystem with sustainable, composable yield infrastructure that can underpin the next generation ofSolana-native financial applications.
Solanausers can instantly access boosted yield opportunities with Nest.
The Plume Nest Points Program is a rewards framework designed to turn participation into ownership. Users can earn Plume Nest Points by holding and using Nest vault tokens. The more users hold and deploy, the more they earn as part of the Season One program.
Solanausers will also soon unlock boosted yield opportunities with direct access to lending markets on Loopscale. These platform integrations allow leverage

# ![Logo_Purple_w bg.png](https://ml.globenewswire.com/Resource/Download/33b684d4-4115-45ef-849e-b207935b0b70?size=3)
# Figure and Leading Crypto Partners Launch RWA Consortium for Onchain Finance on Solana
December 04, 2025 09:00 ET| Source:[Figure Technology Solutions, Inc.](https://www.globenewswire.com/en/search/organization/FT%20Intermediate%CE%B4%20Inc%C2%A7)FollowFigure Technology Solutions, Inc.
Share
* * * * * * * * ![Figure and Leading Crypto Partners Launch RWA Consortium for Onchain Finance on Solana](https://ml.globenewswire.com/Resource/Download/38b97e43-d99f-4a08-9e38-3577453d7f24/rwa-consortium.png)[](#)
*New coalition advances Figure’s vision of moving capital markets to blockchain, expands real-world yield to Solana, and sets the standard for RWA collaboration across chains*
SAN FRANCISCO, Dec. 04, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) --[Figure Technology Solutions, Inc.](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=sVrR0nCGbyoSGqLpmp3W226XOcDwCfx8V3EteYCwLGxXEo0igM8ivJlwMreqY9enDApKlqpkq2h9LGXlpppDneFNwYs_wf55flepAb6tunwEmh-xksGmM2ldyKQcRF4H)(“Figure”)(Nasdaq: FIGR), the leading blockchain-native capital marketplace for the origination, funding, sale, and trading of on-chain loan products and tokenized real-world assets (RWAs), today announced the launch of an RWA consortium, a landmark coalition that will expand access to Figure's more than $1 billion in monthly on-chain loan originations, starting on Solana. The consortium represents a coordinated effort by leading crypto platforms to support and drive adoption of PRIME, the liquid staking token built on the[Hastra liquidity protocol](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=4qB14oW0HW3wV6G7eIeftmMVA8az3rMOK8PAa0Y2-BRXRv-AIPxXC8jou5QwqZswqZL167DOJEXxgNXklTx7X5zNuk4nN-6TXUv6pOrCjxitPRgZlNKhBddz_5Xu1T9Hu1XH4kRiKqkttkiJeicO8MN-CNu8gmEyyquNATcxaPNC56uSFgFkmxuV7i1s-QvGo4s2-XxOi1gshATrL0nmDL5c78zLpeU5MWvBvHd1TKM=), developed in partnership with Figure and the Provenance Blockchain Foundation. PRIME is powered by the Democratized Prime decentralized lending protocol on the Provenance Blockchain, which allows users to lend against pools of tokenized Figure RWA loans. Through this coalition, everyday DeFi users can now access institutional-grade yields previously reserved for banks and accredited investors.
The RWA Consortium builds on Figure’s vision to bring capital markets on-chain, a goal most recently reinforced during its IPO and the expansion of its RWA ecosystem across multiple blockchains. By extending beyond the Provenance Blockchain to integrate with Layer 1 networks like Solana, Figure is partnering with Hastra to leverage Chainlink[CCIP](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=AoAEgzHaPbSD8H81yfVKVDXA32VlG1HlMnUL10_xbh1thIeREQF8WzIHuEqW8sPg7s78VxMYQfWySLEd-xz4Tg==)to power the cross-chain interoperability needed for a connected, multi-chain finance ecosystem. With over $19 billion in on-chain loan originations to date and a[70% market share](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=_8YSDjYvrN99A8tUsqQH01Y3JxsV6a895BAreWpCDDEb82mue5e6XzzI_TsT7oYru-CPY3g5Xe0uEA769WrIxfR1KhHLCDiIcXYiXdT8nwu4hb9Amh8nUkhSFupuvO2d)in the RWA private credit, Figure is uniquely positioned to bridge traditional credit markets with DeFi.
This initiative follows the recent debut of Hastra, a liquidity protocol incubated by Figure and Provenance Blockchain that allows the yields generated in Democratized Prime to reach decentralized finance, with all Hastra yield products secured by the Chainlink data standard. Hastra acts as a

tags.impact_reason: The keynote discusses institutional allocation strategies for digital assets, specifically mentioning Solana's growth, but lacks immediate, direct impact on the Solana ecosystem itself.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a precision balance scale as the Semantic Anchor because the story is about institutional portfolio optimization—the act of carefully balancing assets to improve returns (Sharpe ratio). The scale visually represents the core concept of a '60/40 portfolio' being re-balanced by a small but powerful crypto allocation. The contrast between a large, traditional weight and a small, glowing crystalline weight perfectly illustrates the key data point: a 1-5% allocation having a significant impact. I included the diegetic text '1-5%' as a holographic projection from the crypto asset itself, directly referencing the optimal allocation percentage mentioned in the standfirst and making the core data point the hero of the image.
visual_idea.description: A sleek, modern precision balance scale, crafted from polished brass and obsidian, sits in a minimalist, high-tech laboratory. On the left pan, a large, heavy weight composed of stacked marble and brass discs represents a traditional 60/40 portfolio. On the right pan, a much smaller, glowing crystalline cube, representing digital assets, brings the scale into perfect equilibrium. This cube emits a soft blue light, revealing intricate digital circuits within. Projected from the cube as a sharp, stable hologram is the text '1-5%'. The background is a dark, out-of-focus server room, with deep depth of field and soft, blurred data lights.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A monumental, sleek, modern precision balance scale, exquisitely crafted from highly reflective, polished brass with subtle ambient reflections and deep, slightly reflective obsidian, sits prominently in a minimalist, high-tech laboratory. The composition emphasizes height and epic scale, with ample negative space at the top, presented in a vertical 3:4 aspect ratio. On the left pan, a large, heavy weight, composed of stacked, textured marble and polished brass discs, gleams with subtle reflections, representing a traditional 60/40 portfolio. On the right pan, a much smaller, luminous crystalline cube, translucent and emitting a soft blue internal light, represents digital assets, bringing the scale into perfect equilibrium. The cube's soft blue glow reveals intricate, finely detailed digital circuits within, casting a gentle illumination on the surrounding scale. Projected sharply and stably from the cube is a vibrant, high-contrast, spatially correct holographic display, floating directly above, showing the text '1-5%'. The background is a dark, out-of-focus server room, rendered with deep depth of field, featuring soft, blurred data lights that recede into the distance. The entire scene is bathed in soft commercial product lighting with global illumination, highlighting every hyper-detailed surface, reflection, and texture, creating a visually rich and precise aesthetic. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, physically based rendering (PBR), cinematic lighting.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a precision balance scale as the Semantic Anchor because the story is about institutional portfolio optimization—the act of carefully balancing assets to improve returns (Sharpe ratio). The scale visually represents the core concept of a '60/40 portfolio' being re-balanced by a small but powerful crypto allocation. The contrast between a large, traditional weight and a small, glowing crystalline weight perfectly illustrates the key data point: a 1-5% allocation having a significant impact. I included the diegetic text '1-5%' as a holographic projection from the crypto asset itself, directly referencing the optimal allocation percentage mentioned in the standfirst and making the core data point the hero of the image.
visual_idea.description: A dramatic macro shot focusing on the small, glowing crystalline cube resting on the polished brass pan of the balance scale. The camera is extremely close, highlighting the intricate, luminous digital pathways inside the crystal and the subtle imperfections on the metallic surface of the pan. The background is completely blurred into a dark, clean bokeh, emphasizing the material contrast between the futuristic asset and the traditional instrument. NO TEXT is visible.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A dramatic macro shot, tightly framed and centrally focused, showcasing a small, glowing crystalline cube resting on the highly polished brass pan of a traditional balance scale. The 1:1 square composition fills the frame with exquisite detail and texture. The camera is extremely close, rendering ultra-photorealistic detail, emphasizing the intricate, luminous digital pathways and holographic data glowing within the crystal's core. Subtle imperfections and a rich metallic sheen are visible on the reflective surface of the polished brass pan, contrasting with the futuristic crystal. The background is completely blurred into a dark, clean, high-contrast bokeh, drawing absolute focus to the material interplay between the luminous, precision instrument elements. No text or numbers are visible. 8k, octane render, raytracing, physically based rendering (PBR), hyper-detailed, global illumination, clean studio lighting, large diffused softbox, ambient occlusion.

editorial.headline: EXPAAM Reveals Optimal Crypto Allocation Strategies for Institutional Portfolios
editorial.standfirst: New data suggests a 1-5% digital asset allocation significantly improves Sharpe ratios in standard 60/40 portfolios with negligible volatility impact. The asset manager introduced a comprehensive six-bucket taxonomy to help institutions map traditional mandates to on-chain strategies.
key_takeaways: Crypto user growth is outpacing the early internet by double, projected to reach 3-4 billion users by 2030.
Allocating up to 5% to Bitcoin, Ethereum, or Solana enhances portfolio efficiency by 1-4% without breaching institutional volatility tolerances.
Solana-specific funds are now capturing unique alpha via atomic settlement and on-chain order books impossible in traditional finance.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
The narrative for institutional adoption has shifted from "why invest" to "how to allocate," with **EXPAAM** Partner **Pierre Henry** presenting a compelling quantitative case for digital assets in multi-asset portfolios. Despite the asset class arguably being only 3% into its adoption curve, the growth metrics are staggering: crypto user bases have expanded at 137% annually between 2016 and 2021—double the rate of the early internet. For institutional Limited Partners (LPs), the math is becoming undeniable. Integrating a 1% to 5% sleeve of blue-chip assets like **Bitcoin**, **Ethereum**, or **Solana** into a traditional 60/40 portfolio pushes out the efficient frontier, offering superior risk-adjusted returns (Sharpe and Sortino ratios) while maintaining volatility levels within standard investment committee tolerances.

However, the primary barrier remains the disconnect between interest and execution, largely due to a lack of understanding regarding the "menu of options." **EXPAAM** argues that volatility should not be viewed as a deterrent but as the raw material for alpha generation. By utilizing a "barbell approach"—combining liquid, high-cap assets for beta with smaller-cap, high-convexity tokens for growth—institutions can manage cycle risk effectively. The firm introduced a rigorous taxonomy for allocators, categorizing strategies into six buckets ranging from Directional Spot and Active Liquid Hedge Funds to Market Neutral yield farming and Private Venture. This framework allows LPs to express specific views, whether they are seeking defensive income via staking yields or aggressive growth via illiquid tokens.

Crucially, the unique architecture of high-performance chains is birthing entirely new asset management strategies. **Henry** highlighted that **Solana** is enabling funds to capture alpha that simply does not exist in traditional finance (TradFi). Strategies leveraging atomic settlement and on-chain central limit order books (CLOBs) allow for capital efficiency and arbitrage opportunities that are structurally impossible in slower settlement environments. As regulatory frameworks like MiCA and the U.S. bipartisan innovation agenda provide clearer guardrails, the operational risks regarding custody and counterparty exposure are diminishing, clearing the path for a massive rotation of capital into these sophisticated on-chain instruments.
